reviews.author: Bridget
reviews.text: One of the most unique temples I’ve visited in Vietnam. The architecture is very colorful and detailed — a mix of different religious influences all in one place. It almost looks unreal because of the bright colors and intricate design. The atmosphere felt calm and peaceful, and I was able to walk around and really appreciate the details inside without it feeling crowded. It’s quite far from Ho Chi Minh City, but it’s worth visiting at least once. Very beautiful and very different from other temples I’ve seen.

reviews.author: Mary Fan
reviews.text: Gorgeous cathedral! A fascinating glimpse at the Caodai religion. Super unique art style and architecture. Just know that if you’re taking a tour from Ho Chi Minh City, it’s a good 3-4 hour drive depending on traffic

reviews.author: Dương Trần
reviews.text: Great Divine Temple The Great Divine Temple, also known as the Cao Dai Cathedral (/ˌkaʊ ˈdaɪ/) or the Tay Ninh Holy See (Vietnamese: Tòa Thánh Tây Ninh Vietnamese pronunciation: [twaː˨˩ tʰan˦˥ təj˧˧ nɨn˧˧]), is a religious building in the Cao Dai Holy See complex in Tây Ninh province, Southeast Vietnam. It is the first and most important temple of Caodaism in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Ms Lem
reviews.text: Interesting church to visit, caodaism . It is a religious comprising several religious, like Taoism, catholic. Each staircase represent 5-10 rules and maximum you can get is 9th staircase. You can visit here by joining the local tour from Ho Chi Minh City to Black Virgin Mountain.

reviews.author: Như Nguyễn
reviews.text: The most beautiful structure of our religion- A Vietnamese Great Way- Caodaism . I’m so proud of these things- where I was born

reviews.author: Precin Salvan
reviews.text: It's a place of worship for a new 100 year old religion. It's a mixture of several religions including Taoism, Buddhism, Christianity and several others. And they pray to the eye on the sky which is represented in a triangle.

reviews.author: Angus McColl
reviews.text: The temple is spectacular and we were allowed inside to walk around and take photos. Just stunning inside and outside. The people there were helpful and friendly, and very tolerant of having visitors wandering through their temple.

reviews.author: 黄Jack
reviews.text: A solemn and very spacious place of worship. There are many living areas inside such as schools. The Tay Ninh Holy See is a blend of religious and national cultural elements. In addition to its unique architecture, the Holy See of Tay Ninh is also famous for its religious ceremonies, including prayer ceremonies held at fixed times of the day.

reviews.author: RenOverSea
reviews.text: This is certainly a very unique Church and a must see place. It is known as "The Great Divine Temple," or as the Cao Dai Cathedral, or the Tay Ninh Holy See. It is a religious building in the Cao Dai Holy See complex and the first and most important temple of Caodaism here in Vietnam. This religion was established in 1926, and groundbreaking for this church took place in 1931, but due to insufficient budget, the actual construction did not start until 1936. The building was then finally completed in 1947.

reviews.author: James Ong
reviews.text: The Tay Linh Holy See is located almost in city centre. The compound is very big and full of many religion structures and temple. The building color of orange and yellow are very attractive and eye catching.

reviews.author: Ana / Аня (Photographer)
reviews.text: The temple itself is very beautiful, BUT ! The temple staff behaved terribly. We didn’t know that stepping in certain areas was not allowed, because it was not shown anywhere, but instead of informing us, a staff member started grabbing our hands, pushing us, and being rude. When we told him not to touch us, he continued anyway. What kind of spirituality can there be if the temple staff themselves behave in such an inhumane way? Horrible experience

reviews.author: Mark
reviews.text: Beautiful temple in spacious complex. At lunchtime various ceremonies and prayers. Very impressive.

reviews.author: Sam Asus
reviews.text: Biggest Cao Dai site in Vietnam, a few hectares in size. As all gates are not always opened for car entry, better check with locals which one is allowed if you're self driving. Motorbikes entry are ok. Caution : (i) do not wear shorts if visiting inside the temple, (ii) remove all headgear, (iii) don't stand in the middle of the shrine's alleyway for pictures. Beware of wild monkeys roaming outside, they will steal any shiny objects.

reviews.author: David Lim
reviews.text: Fantastic!!! What an amazing place for a uniquely Vietnamese religion established only about 100 years ago. This is the Vatican of Coa Dai. Unlike the Vatican, there are no stolen treasures or criminals hiding out in the buildings.

reviews.author: Son Nguyen Van
reviews.text: The holy was built in 1931 ( as i asked the holy guardian man) and now it's the common place for Caodaist come to pray and practise for Caodaism. Really nice and meaningful place to visit

reviews.author: Luong Nguyen
reviews.text: The building is great. We do not need to buy ticket for this. And I recommend that you shoud visit this at noon for beautiful photo.

reviews.author: Ray G
reviews.text: This is a religious space, be respectful and dont go tromping all over without permission. If a member of the temple asks you to move or blocks your way it's because you are about to cause offence or some embaressment. The building is huge, the icons and art is spectacular and colourful. Enjoy.

reviews.author: Thiep Mai Phuc
reviews.text: Tay Ninh Holy See, also known as the Holy Palace, is a religious building of the Cao Dai religion. Not only is it an ideal check-in point, Tay Ninh Holy See is also a famous cultural work, converging unique architectural styles. This place is also very close to many other tourist destinations in Tay Ninh, convenient for your travel. One of the unique features of the architecture of Tay Ninh Holy See is the streamlined style of many religious civilizations in the world. Therefore, traveling to Tay Ninh Holy See will definitely be a trip that brings you many cultural and architectural experiences. If you come to Tay Ninh, you should come here, it's peaceful and extremely interesting, the people here are wonderful and friendly, wish you a happy trip!!

reviews.author: Nhu Nguyen
reviews.text: Extremely busy at the buffet on the weekend. I guess it’s summer holiday. Avoid the weekend if you can. Very nice place. Beautiful garden of flowers. I didn’t know there’s so much to see. Make sure you check out the visuals video shown inside the doom house on the top and the laughing Buddha.

reviews.author: Joe Ang
reviews.text: Great temple and we'll maintained since 1947...

reviews.author: Hien Nguyen
reviews.text: This temple surprised me with its colorful and unique architecture blending many religions. Watching the noon prayer ceremony is a breathtaking cultural experience. Visitors should climb to the upper balcony for a great view. I’d tell my friends this is a must-see spiritual spot near Ho Chi Minh.

reviews.author: Ellie Tran
reviews.text: A beautiful and peaceful place with unique architecture and rich spiritual culture. The Cao Dai Holy See in Tay Ninh is truly worth a visit to learn more about Vietnam’s diverse religious traditions.

reviews.author: Ulla Richards
reviews.text: Well worth a visit. Unable to take pictures inside as there was a service, but the inside decoration is as spectacular as the outside!

reviews.author: Ailin Goh
reviews.text: Must visit if you are in Tay Ninh.

reviews.author: Malory Columbretis
reviews.text: It was a scorching noon when we went here, so I was too busy sweating my arse off to actually enjoy the place, but I appreciate it. The cathedral is majestic and colorful. The religion itself seems very interesting to me. The murals, landscaping, architecture and planning are a marvel to look at. The temple grounds is amazingly vast too. It is a great place for tourists to take pictures.

reviews.author: rand chua
reviews.text: A great temple of the Holy See. Do come visit and pray 🙏. Do come before the praying time to avoid disappointment. Do come on their special Holy Day occasion to see it happening. Do Bring own snack and drink or purchase outside the temple area.

reviews.author: Carsten Paulsen
reviews.text: Very beautiful and intresting - even for a non-budist like me 🤭

reviews.author: Vincent Oliver
reviews.text: The Holy See is a major site for Caodaism, where the religion was started in Tây Ninh, but best appreciated during the cooler times of the day. The main prayer hall, The Great Divine Temple, is impressive both outside and inside. Also walk down the roadway to the gardens with many potted plants and flowers. Be careful with small children or food as there are many monkeys in the wooden park and they roam onto the grass and paved areas.

reviews.author: gabs1138 o
reviews.text: Weird and interesting place with a ceremony during 35 mn. You can listen traditional music and songs. There are monkeys in the park near by.

reviews.author: Yew Hing Yap
reviews.text: Too many restrictions inside the temple as if we are criminals in their holy place. Most unfriendly temple visited in my lifetime. They might as well reject visitors.
